name: Humbaba
description: Humbaba, also known as Huwawa, was a monstrous guardian of the Cedar Forest in Mesopotamian mythology, appointed by the god Enlil. He was a fearsome being, possessing immense strength and terrifying features. Humbaba's roar was like a flood, his breath was death, and his steps shook the earth. He was tasked with protecting the sacred cedar trees from mortals, and he fiercely defended his domain. Gilgamesh and Enkidu, driven by ambition and a desire for glory, ventured into the Cedar Forest to challenge Humbaba. Despite the warnings of the elders, they pressed on, determined to defeat the monster and harvest the valuable cedar wood. The battle was long and arduous, but with the help of the god Shamash, Gilgamesh and Enkidu were ultimately able to overcome Humbaba. His death marked a turning point in their journey, leading to both triumph and tragedy. He serves as a symbol of the dangers of unchecked ambition and the consequences of defying the gods. Humbaba's grotesque appearance and terrifying power made him a formidable adversary and a memorable figure in Mesopotamian lore, representing the untamed forces of nature and the divine authority that controlled them.
subtitle: Guardian of the Cedar Forest
appearance: Humbaba's appearance is described as grotesque and terrifying. He had the face of a lion, intestines for entrails, and a body covered in scales or plates. His roar was like a flood, and his breath was said to bring death. Some accounts depict him with claws and fangs, further emphasizing his monstrous nature. Other descriptions suggest a more human-like form but still possessing immense strength and a frightening demeanor.
habitat: Cedar Forest in the mountains of Lebanon/Syria (though the exact location is debated)
behavior: Humbaba was fiercely protective of the Cedar Forest, acting as Enlil's appointed guardian. He was known for his rage and ferocity, attacking anyone who dared to enter his domain. He was a solitary creature, existing solely to protect the sacred trees. While terrifying, some interpretations suggest he was a servant bound by his duty.

keyMyths.title: The Epic of Gilgamesh
keyMyths.description: Gilgamesh and Enkidu venture into the Cedar Forest to slay Humbaba and harvest cedar wood. They ultimately succeed with the help of the sun god Shamash, but the act has serious consequences.

sources.author: Unknown
